methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $400M last post money.

data.narrative:
AiDash is a Series C-stage infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $91M+ to date and carries a last post-money valuation of $400M+. The valuation analysis is constrained by an absence of public comparables and identified private peers in the infrastructure and logistics sector, limiting the scope of triangulation available.

Given the limited comp universe, this analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to later-stage transitions. Applied to the $400M last post-money valuation, this method implies a valuation of $680M. The stage-progression approach is grounded in empirical patterns of growth between funding rounds but carries model risk in the absence of sector-specific validation.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a range of $578M to $782M, with a median of $680M. The width of this range reflects inherent uncertainty in extrapolating forward from a single methodology. Key sensitivities include: (1) the appropriate step-up multiplier for infrastructure-focused venture stages, which may differ materially from cross-sector norms; (2) the absence of peer valuation data to benchmark growth expectations; and (3) market conditions for later-stage venture financing in infrastructure. Procurement of private comparable transactions or expansion of the comp set would materially improve confidence in the valuation range.
